channel.title: Cointelegraph.com News
channel.description: Cointelegraph covers fintech, blockchain and Bitcoin bringing you the latest crypto news and analyses on the future of money.

image.title: Cointelegraph.com News
image.description: Cointelegraph covers fintech, blockchain and Bitcoin bringing you the latest crypto news and analyses on the future of money.

item.title: XRP price trades in ‘value zone’ near $1.40 as whales pull $170M from exchanges
item.description: Whales withdrew $170 million in XRP from Binance as the price holds a key $1.35-$1.40 accumulation and support zone.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Nancy Lubale

item.title: UAE-linked ADI Chain gains Ledger support amid stablecoin growth
item.description: The integration gives ADI token holders access to Ledger’s self-custody platform as ADI Chain expands its stablecoin and tokenized asset network.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Nate Kostar

item.title: Crypto PAC money pours into Texas primary runoffs, as prediction markets favor challengers
item.description: The crypto-aligned Protect Progress PAC reported spending another $750,000 on media for a Democratic candidate over Representative Al Green, whom it described as “actively hostile” to digital assets.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Turner Wright

item.title: CoinQuant introduces trading infrastructure for the agent economy
item.description: CoinQuant, the AI-powered no-code trading platform that has attracted over 15,000 users since launch, today announces its expansion into a unified trading intelligence architecture built for both human traders and autonomous AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: Squid and Safe Labs say third-party module behind $3.2M exploit
item.description: A third-party module drained about $3 million from Safe wallets, with Squid attributing the incident to an external Safe module, saying its core systems were unaffected.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Helen Partz

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Cointelegraph

item.title: ARIQO makes its Bangkok debut at SEABW, drawing industry attention
item.description: Canton Foundation, Toss, BitGo Among Co-Hosts at Private Event; Token Launch Slated for Second Half of 2026
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: Coinbase CEO’s finance wishlist mirrors company’s product roadmap
item.description: Brian Armstrong's wishlist for upgrading global finance aligns with the exchange's push into stocks, prediction markets and stablecoin payments, though some priorities remain aspirational.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Christina Comben

item.title: Ethereum whale opens $100M short as Vitalik Buterin vows to 'sell less ETH'
item.description: ETH’s rebound toward the whale’s liquidation zone near $2,150 puts its short at risk of over $1 million in potential losses.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Yashu Gola

item.title: Paper losses and scrapped ETFs. What Trump Media’s 2,650 BTC transfer really means
item.description: Digital asset treasuries (DATs) and the broader practice of building corporate Bitcoin reserves became hugely popular in 2024 and 2025, thanks in large part to the success of Strategy, whose "flywheel" many tried to replicate.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by

item.title: Cyannova Capital announces global launch at its first strategic reception in Hong Kong
item.description: Cyannova Capital (“Cyannova” or the “Company”), a New York-based investment firm, announces its inaugural investment fund, Cyannova Capital, LP, at its private industry reception held in Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: Satoshi-era Bitcoin miner transfers $203M in BTC to OTC desks
item.description: A Satoshi-era Bitcoin miner moved 2,650 BTC worth about $203 million to FalconX and Cumberland, while retaining 6,000 BTC.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Zoltan Vardai

item.title: Bitcoin risks drop to $72K as demand metric hits 2026 lows
item.description: Bitcoin’s weakening demand failed to absorb increased selling pressure, raising risks of a further BTC price drop toward $72,000.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Nancy Lubale

item.title: Indonesia blocks Polymarket after bets on president’s exit
item.description: Indonesia blocked Polymarket after users bet on President Prabowo Subianto leaving office early, citing gambling concerns and expanding global scrutiny of prediction markets.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Helen Partz

item.title: Crypto lobby spending on Republicans far outpaces Democratic support
item.description: The crypto lobby has spent tens of millions of dollars more on Republican candidates and elections than on Democratic lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Aaron Wood

item.title: BTC price to attack $80K shorts on Iran peace deal: Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
item.description: Bitcoin traders predicted a short squeeze to $80,000, but a lack of overall demand and the return of leverage sparked warnings of more liquidation events to come.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by William Suberg

item.title: Influence360 launches as the first AI & data-driven Web3 KOL platform with global KOL coverage and real attribution
item.description: Influence360 today announced the launch of its platform, introducing a new infrastructure layer for Web3 influencer marketing built around trust, data, and global execution.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: New York lawsuit tests lost property claim over dormant Bitcoin
item.description: A New York lawsuit seeks ownership of 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, raising questions over lost crypto, private keys and property law.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Zoltan Vardai

item.title: Tether, Georgia plan lari-backed stablecoin GELT under new rules
item.description: Georgia’s central bank released stablecoin rules in March covering reserve backing, issuer documents and external auditor verification.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Ezra Reguerra

item.title: Crypto entrepreneur Chun Wang joins SpaceX mission to Mars
item.description: Chun Wang, who bankrolled and led a SpaceX flight over Earth’s poles, says he will head to Mars on SpaceX’s first manned mission to the red planet.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Stephen Katte

item.title: Bitcoin ETFs' 6 day loss streak pushes market closer to net outflows for 2026
item.description: Dek: US Bitcoin ETF net inflows have shrunk to $536 million so far in 2026, after recording a six-day run of net outflows totalling $1.55 billion.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Brayden Lindrea

item.title: ‘TrapDoor’ malware targets crypto dev tools in supply chain attack
item.description: Socket says a campaign of malicious packages is aiming to steal crypto and is injecting hidden instructions that hijack popular AI coding assistants.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Martin Young

item.title: ‘Developed ecosystem’ based on crypto has sprung up for AI agents: Report
item.description: Stablecoins became the default settlement layer for AI agents as crypto payment rails can handle sub-dollar transactions more efficiently, says a report from Keyrock.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Stephen Katte

item.title: Kalshi backs prediction markets lobby group with former Trump official
item.description: "We’re not going to be outspent or out-organized by entrenched interests protecting their monopolies," said John Bivona, head of government relations at Kalshi.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Martin Young

item.title: SEC postpones plan allowing 'innovation exemption' for tokenized stocks: Report
item.description: The SEC has reportedly delayed releasing a proposal permitting tokenized stock trading after receiving industry concerns.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Brayden Lindrea

item.title: Soaring bond prices signal 'structural' shift and Bitcoin 'supercycle': Analyst
item.description: Fixed-income investors are in a "panic" as government securities, once seen as low-risk, begin to crack, according to BitMEX researcher Shang Wu.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Vince Quill

item.title: Buterin fires back at Ethereum Foundation critics, recommits to neutrality
item.description: The Ethereum Foundation holds less than 1% of all ETH in circulation while other protocol foundations typically hold 10-50% of their native token's supply, the founder said.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Vince Quill

item.title: FTX law firm Fenwick & West to pay $54M to victims in settlement
item.description: The law firm agreed to a settlement in February 2026 and is facing a separate $525 million lawsuit over its role in the collapse of the FTX crypto exchange.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Vince Quill

item.title: Tom Lee’s Ethereum portfolio down $7.35B as ETH price outlook worsens
item.description: ETH’s bearish chart setup points to a 25% drop toward $1,600, risking over $10 billion in paper losses for BitMine in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Yashu Gola

item.title: CFTC officials who questioned prediction markets were suspended: NYT
item.description: A New York Times investigation found that senior CFTC officials who raised concerns about Polymarket, Crypto.com and Gemini were suspended and pushed out.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Amin Haqshanas
